name: Path of Cabo de Busto — circular
name_original: Senda del Cabo de Busto — circular
region_intro: Hiking around Valdés explores a region characterized by its rugged coastline, green valleys, and river gorges in Asturias, Spain. The landscape features a mix of coastal paths offering sea views and inland trails winding through forests and along riverbanks. Elevation changes vary from gentle coastal strolls to more challenging ascents in the interior. This diverse terrain provides a range of outdoor experiences for hikers. Best hiking trails around Valdés The most popular hiking route is Path of Cabo de Busto — circular, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 21 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic coastal views around the cape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Luarca and Northern Route Loop, a moderate 7.1 miles (11.4 km) path. This trail explores the surroundings of Luarca, combining coastal sections with inland paths. Local hikers also love the Hoces del Esva I Trail, a 10.1 miles (16.3 km) trail leading through the Esva river gorge, often completed in about 5 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Valdés is defined by coastal paths, river gorges, and forested trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more difficult, longer routes. The routes in Valdés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Valdés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Valdés?
A: Valdés offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 60 routes available. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Valdés?
A: Hiking in Valdés, Asturias, Spain, allows you to explore a diverse landscape featuring rugged coastlines with stunning sea views, lush green valleys, and dramatic river gorges. You'll find a mix of coastal paths and inland trails winding through forests and along riverbanks.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Valdés?
A: Yes, Valdés has plenty of easy trails. Approximately 32 of the routes are classified as easy, making them suitable for beginners or families looking for a relaxed outdoor experience. An example of an easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14985825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte del Cabo Viewpoint – Punta Arenosa Viewpoint loop from Bustu&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Valdés?
A: Yes, Valdés features several circular routes, which are great for exploring without retracing your steps. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047542514/path-of-cabo-de-busto-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path of Cabo de Busto — circular&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic coastal views.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Valdés?
A: The hiking trails in Valdés are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 140 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from coastal paths to river gorges, and the well-maintained routes that offer diverse experiences.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls in Valdés?
A: Yes, Valdés is home to several beautiful waterfalls that can be incorporated into your hikes. You can visit highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/865581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nonaya Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2540246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oneta Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4085144" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Fero Waterfall (Barayo River)&lt;/a&gt;. For more options, check out the guide to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3825587/waterfalls-around-valdes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfalls around Valdés&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural monuments can I find along the trails?
A: Valdés offers a rich array of landmarks and natural monuments. Along your hikes, you might encounter the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/363182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Regalina Chapel&lt;/a&gt;, the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3710397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luarca Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/93692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of Silence&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4999971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vidio Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; also offers spectacular views. For more, explore the guide to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3928990/natural-monuments-around-valdes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Monuments around Valdés&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging or difficult hikes in Valdés?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Valdés offers 10 difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048597131/hoces-del-esva-i-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Esva I Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through the Esva river gorge and involves significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Valdés?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the diverse landscapes of Valdés, with its coastal paths and inland forests, suggest that hiking is enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer is ideal for coastal walks. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any long-distance trails or multi-day hiking options in Valdés?
A: While the guide focuses on day hikes, some routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048392424/northern-route-luarca-and-barayo-reserve-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Northern Route: Luarca and Barayo Reserve Loop&lt;/a&gt; are quite long, extending over 24 km. These longer routes can be challenging and may require more planning, potentially offering opportunities for extended exploration within a single day or as part of a multi-day itinerary if combined with local accommodation.

Q: Can I find trails that offer panoramic viewpoints in Valdés?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Valdés provide excellent viewpoints, especially those along the rugged coastline or ascending into the interior. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14985825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte del Cabo Viewpoint – Punta Arenosa Viewpoint loop from Bustu&lt;/a&gt; is a great example of a route designed to showcase scenic vistas.
tips: Spectacular views of the coastal landscape and the Valdés mountains.
